FT_IC1.evidence: Analyzes U.S. ABCD Study data, Year 3 follow-up (2019-2021), 21 geographically diverse U.S. sites, N=10,092 adolescents aged 11-15.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cross-sectional analysis of ABCD Year 3 data; reports descriptive statistics and Chi-square/t-test comparisons of social media use patterns.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Academic Pediatrics; no duplicate identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: All numeric estimates pair SM/phone variables with demographic variables only. Comparisons of platform use, SMAQ score, number of accounts, and SM time are stratified exclusively by age group (under 13 vs 13+) and sex (male vs female). SMAQ mean score by sex (1.98 vs 2.19, p<0.001) has SMAQ — an SM variable — as the DV and sex as the predictor; both sides are SM variable or demographic. No analysis pairs any SM/phone variable with an individual-trait-level outcome (mental health, sleep, substance use, cognition, etc.). NOT_MET: other side of every analysis is demographic-only.

notes: Purely descriptive prevalence paper characterizing SM platform use and SMAQ by age and sex. Every comparison is demographic-stratified; no individual-trait outcome is analyzed. Excluded under FT-EC2.

FT_IC1.evidence: Prospective cohort data from the U.S. ABCD Study (Years 2-3), N=8006, recruited from 21 U.S. sites in 2016-2018.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original prospective empirical analysis; logistic regression models predicting substance use experimentation from screen modalities.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Drug and Alcohol Dependence; no duplicate identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 hit: social media (hours/day) as IV prospectively predicts substance use experimentation (any, alcohol, nicotine, cannabis) with AOR 1.20 (95% CI 1.14-1.26) for any substance; texting AOR 1.18 (95% CI 1.12-1.24); video chat AOR 1.09 (95% CI 1.03-1.16). Named SM/phone-specific variables (social media, texting, video chatting) paired with individual-trait outcomes (substance use experimentation). MET at Step 1.

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple logistic regression ORs with 95% CIs reported for social media, texting, and video chat vs each substance use outcome (Fig. 1 forest plot).

FT_IC4.evidence: Inferential analysis of SM/phone variable (social media time, texting, video chat) predicting individual-trait outcome (substance use experimentation) with quantitative results reported.

FT_EC7_reason: Design is longitudinal but SM/phone variables (social media, texting, video chat) are the IV predicting substance use as DV. EC7 requires SM as DV-only; here SM is IV. EC7 does not apply.
notes: Clean INCLUDE. Prospective ABCD study; three named phone/SM modalities (social media, texting, video chat) as IVs predicting substance use experimentation as outcome.

FT_IC1.evidence: Longitudinal data from U.S. ABCD Study (N=7691, ages 9-10 at baseline 2016-2018), follow-up Years 2, 3, and 4; 21 U.S. sites.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original prospective cohort mediation analysis using generalized structural equation modeling (GSEM) of ABCD data.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed short communication in Drug and Alcohol Dependence; no duplicate identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 hit: social media time (hours/day, Year 2) as IV directly predicts cannabis experimentation (Year 4) with direct effect B=0.18 (95% CI: 0.13-0.24, p<0.001). Named SM variable (social media time) paired with individual-trait outcome (cannabis experimentation/substance use). MET at Step 1.

FT_IC3.evidence: Path coefficients (B), indirect effect sizes (positive expectancy indirect B=0.05, 95% CI: 0.03-0.06; negative expectancy indirect B=0.03, 95% CI: 0.02-0.04), and mediation percentages reported.

FT_IC4.evidence: GSEM path coefficients and indirect effects quantify the SM-cannabis experimentation association; quantitative numeric results reported throughout.

FT_EC7_reason: SM (social media time, Year 2) is the IV in all analyses, predicting cannabis experimentation (Year 4) as DV. EC7 requires SM as DV-only with no SM-as-IV analysis; here SM is IV. EC7 does not apply.
notes: Clean INCLUDE. Prospective mediation analysis; social media time as IV, cannabis experimentation as DV, cannabis expectancies as mediator. Individual-trait outcome (substance use) on other side.
